text: Noko ɛnee nzule vi azɛlɛ bo finde bu azɛlɛ ne azo amuala tɛloo.
translated: If you do not know the way to the river, you will get lost in the forest.

ref: So the Lord took some soil and made animals and birds. He brought them to the man to see what names he would give each of them. Then the man named the tame animals and the birds and the wild animals. That's how they got their names. None of these was the right kind of partner for the man.
text: Sonla totonle nyɛmoa ne mɔ aluma ngoko ngoko, ɔtotonle nloma mɔɔ tu dua anwomanyunlu la aluma yɛɛ ɔtotonle nanemgbole mɔɔ wɔ ɛbɔ nu la aluma. Na ɔdaye sonla ɛdeɛ, yeannyia ye ɛsaso boavolɛ biala.
translated: The hen lays eggs and sits on them, she lays eggs on the ground and sits on them until they hatch. But if the hen dies, we do not get any chicks at all.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: Ɛdɛnkɛma Nyamenle vale bowule ne mɔɔ ɔyele la ɔyɛle sonla raalɛ na ɔvale ye ɔrɛlɛle nrenya ne.
translated: Because God loves us so much, He gave His only Son so that whoever believes in Him will not perish.

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Yemɔti a nrenya fi ɔ ze nee ɔ nli anwo na ɔfa ɔ nwo ɔbɔ ɔ ye anwo kyengye, na bɛ mu nwiɔ bɛbɔ nu bɛyɛ nwonane ko a.
translated: We should not take something from someone and then turn around to use it against them, because in the end, it may come back to harm us as well.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Nzinlii mɔɔ aleɛ ne bɔle ɔ nloa aze la, bɛdele Ɛdɛnkɛma Nyamenle agyakɛ abo kɛ ɔlɛkpɔsa tola ne anu, na bɛnriandile bɛhɔveale tola ne anu mbaka ne mɔ anu, amaa yeannwu bɛ.
translated: The words we speak and the actions we take are all seen by Almighty God, who watches over us and listens to our words, so let us always speak good words, so that we may live well.

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Akee ɔzele raalɛ ne kɛ, “Mebamaa wɔade nyane kpalɛ wɔ awolɛ nu; ɛbade nyane wɔ wɔ ralɛ ɛwolɛ nu, noko ɛ nwo balɔ ɛ hu na yeazie wɔ.”
translated: Then the elder said, “I have told you this thing many times; if I tell you again and you do not listen, it will not be good for you.”

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Mbaka nee ndile mɔɔ anwo le mbowule la bafifi azɛlɛ ye azo, noko ɛbɔ nu ala a ɛbadidi a.
translated: This child does not sleep at night because of mosquitoes, so we need to buy a mosquito net for him.

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Akee Ɛdɛnkɛma Nyamenle zele kɛ, “Bɛnlea, kɛkala sonla ɛyɛ kɛ yɛ nuhua ko la, yenwu kpalɛ nee ɛtane. Saa yɛannea ye boɛ a ɔbali ngoane baka ne ɔdaye ye ma ne bie na yeadɛnla ngoane nu dahuu.”
translated: Then God said, “Behold, the man has become like one of us, knowing good and evil. Now, lest he reach out his hand and take also from the tree of life and eat, and live forever.”

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: Ɛdɛnkɛma Nyamenle voanle sonla vile tola ne anu, na ɔvale tease abɔdeɛma nee dadeɛ mɔɔ ɛlɛsɔ na ɔpɛnepɛne ɔ nwo ɛleka biala la ɔrazinzale Yidɛn tola ne aduduleɛ adenle ne mɔɔ kɔ ngoane baka ne anwo la.
translated: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth, and the earth was without form and empty, and darkness was upon the face of the deep, and the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!
text: Asoo saa ɛyɛle ye boɛ a, anrɛɛ ɛ nyunlu ɛnrɛra anyia? Na wɔanyɛ ye boɛ la, ɛtane ɛhɔ ɛ nwo nzia; ɔkpondɛ kɛ ɔfo ɛ nwo zo, na wɔmɔ yɛɛ ɔwɔ kɛ ɛfo ɔ nwo zo a.”
translated: If this is what you want, why then do you not do it? And if you do not want it, leave it there; the owner and the thief will meet there, and whoever is stronger will take it.

ref: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let's go for a walk.” And when they were out in a field, Cain attacked and killed him.
text: Ɛhye anzi Keen zele ɔ diema Ebɛle kɛ, “Maa yɛhɔ ɛbɔ nu.” Bɛhɔle la, Keen holole ɔ diema Ebɛle anwo zo na ɔhunle ye.
translated: It happened that Cain said to Abel, “Let us go to the field.” While they were there, Cain rose up against Abel his brother and killed him.

ref: Later, Cain and his wife had a son named Enoch. At the time Cain was building a town, and so he named it Enoch after his son.
text: Keen vale ɔ ye na ɔ ye wole ralɛ nrenya, bɛdonle ye Yinɔko. Keen kyekyele suakpole ko na ɔvale ɔ ra ne duma ɔdole zolɛ.
translated: Keen is a person who is always happy, everyone calls him Yinɔko. Keen likes to play and he always does his work well.

ref: One day, Lamech said to his two wives, “A young man wounded me, and I killed him.
text: Leemɛke zele ye mraalɛ ne mɔ kɛ, “Eda nee Zela, bɛdie me aneɛ; me ye mɔ, bɛdie mɔɔ melɛka la: Mehu kpavolɛ bie mɔɔ bɔle me, bodale me la.
translated: The child looked at his mother and said, “Today, Mother Zela, give me food; I am hungry, give me something to eat: I have not eaten anything since morning, please help me.”

ref: Anyone who tries to get even with me will be punished ten times more than anyone who tries to get even with Cain.”
text: Saa mɔɔ kɛhu Keen la bɛkɛli ye nzenralɛ fane nsuu a, ɛnee mɔɔ kɛhu medame Leemɛke la, bɛkɛli ye nzenralɛ fane abulasuu nee nsuu.” Sɛte nee Yinɔhye
translated: If someone from Keen drinks water from the river, and someone from Leemeke drinks water from the well, it is the same water.

ref: Adam and his wife had another son. They named him Seth, because they said, “God has given us a son to take the place of Abel, who was killed by his brother Cain.”
text: Adam nee ɔ ye wole ralɛ nrenya bieko. Ɔ ye hanle kɛ, “Nyamenle ɛmaa me ralɛ nrenya ɛmaa meva mezie Ebɛle, mɔɔ Keen hunle ye la abo zo.” Na ɔdonle ye Sɛte.
translated: Adam and his wife had a son. She said, “God has given me a son to replace Abel, because Cain killed him.” And they named him Seth.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Yinɔhye lile ɛvolɛ ɛya ngɔnla ɛvolɛ nnu, na ɔwule.
translated: The sun rises in the east and sets in the west.

ref: and God took him away at the age of
text: Yinɔko nee Nyamenle luale na yeanwu, ɔminliminlinle, ɔboalekɛ Nyamenle vale ye hɔle.
translated: God is the creator of heaven and earth, almighty, and the only true God.

ref: and died at the age of
text: Yemɔti Mɛtusɛla lile ɛvolɛ ɛya ngɔnla abulasia nee ngɔnla, na ɔwule.
translated: We know Methuselah lived for nine hundred and sixty-nine years, and then he died.

ref: and he said, “I'm going to destroy every person on earth! I'll even wipe out animals, birds, and reptiles. I'm sorry I ever made them.”
text: na ɔhanle kɛ, “Mebɔ sonla mɔɔ mebɔ la abo zo bɔkɔɔ wɔ azɛlɛ ye azo. Mesɛkye debie biala mɔɔ mebɔ la, sonla, nane, ninyɛne mɔɔ twenle bɛ nwo aze la, yɛɛ nloma muala. Menlu me nwo bɔbɔ kɛ mebɔ bɛ a.”
translated: "And he said, 'I will go and see what is happening in the town. Every morning I go, see, listen, and wait to hear what is going on, and then I come back. I do not want to stay where I am.'"

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yemɔti fa gɔfa baka bobɔ ɛlɛnemgbole; kyɛ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwolɛ amuala.
translated: We should always speak the truth; but sometimes silence is better than words that may cause trouble.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Na wɔmɔ, me nee wɔ bayɛ ngyekyeleɛ. Ɛ nee ɛ mra mrenya, nee ɛ ye, yɛɛ ɛ mra mrenya mraalɛ ne mɔ bavo ɛlɛne ne anu.
translated: And so, I was with them in the evening. It was during the new moon, and yes, it was during the new moon that we sat together and talked about the past and the future.

ref: Noah took a male and a female of every living creature with him,
text: Ɔvale abɔdeɛ ngakyile ne mɔ mɔɔ die ɛnwomenle la ko biala, nyinli nee belɛ,
translated: The oval object is hard and its inside is white like milk, it is edible, and children like it.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Eza Nowa hendɛle kenle nsuu na ɔzoanle abubule ne bieko; ɛnɛ ɛdeɛ, abubule ne anzia amba.
translated: In the days of Noah, there was a flood that destroyed everything; but now, destruction does not come with water.

ref: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
text: Siane nwiɔ ne anu kenle ne mɔɔ tɔ zolɛ abulanwiɔ nee nsuu la ɛnee akee azɛlɛ nwo ɛyɛ resee kpalɛ.
translated: Wash your hair and face well with clean water every morning and evening to keep your body healthy.

ref: the living creatures left in groups of their own kind. The Lord's Promise for the Earth
text: Bɛvindele nane nee nloma ne mɔ amuala, nee debie biala mɔɔ twenle ɔ nwo aze la, ko biala nee ye ɛsaso hɔle ye ekpunli ekpunli. Nowa bɔ azu
translated: Children like to play and run around, and every day they wait for the sun to rise, so that they can go outside and have fun together. Come and join them.

ref: I am going to make a solemn promise to you and to everyone who will live after you.
text: “Nea, me nee wɔ nee ɛ bo zo amra,
translated: Well, I am here with you now,

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: mebado me nyangondonle ne wɔ anwomanyunlu, na yemɔ yɛɛ bayɛ ngyekyeleɛ ne mɔɔ me nee ewiade amra ɛkyekye la anwo sɛkɛlɛneɛ a.
translated: I will lift up my prayer and song to my God, and I will thank Him for all the good things and the blessings He has given me throughout the day and night.

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: mebahakye ngyekyeleɛ ne mɔɔ me nee wɔ nee abɔdeɛ muala ɛkyekye la kɛ azuyilɛ ɛnrɛzɛkye ewiade abɔdeɛ ne mɔ amuala bieko ɛlɛ.
translated: Good morning, the sun and the moon are among the things that shine in the sky, and today the weather is bright and the sun and the moon are both up.

ref: The rainbow will be the sign of that solemn promise. Noah and His Family
text: Ngyekyeleɛ mɔɔ me nee aleɛabo tease abɔdeɛ muala ɛkyekye la anwo sɛkɛlɛneɛ ɛne.” Nowa nee ɔ mra mrenya ne mɔ
translated: The dove returned to me in the evening with a freshly plucked olive leaf in its beak. So Noah knew that the waters had receded from the earth.

ref: Noah farmed the land and was the first to plant a vineyard.
text: Nowa le ɛyazonlɛnli. Ɔdaye a ɔlumuale ɔzonle ɛya na ɔyɛle vanye egyinli a.
translated: Now is the planting season. The farmer prepares the land so that he can plant his crops.

ref: One day he got drunk and was lying naked in his tent.
text: Ɔnlonle nwanye ne bie na ɔbole, ɔkpale ɔ nwo na ɔhɔlale ye ndanlɛ sua ne anu.
translated: The child listens to his mother, he learns from her and follows her advice to become wise.

ref: After the flood Shem, Ham, and Japheth had many descendants. The Descendants of Japheth
text: Nowa amra mrenya Hyɛm, Ham, yɛɛ Gyeefɛte nee bɛ bo zo amra awolɛndoazo ne mɔ ɛne: Azuyilɛ ne anzi Nowa amra ne mɔ wole mralɛ mrenya.
translated: Noah had three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and from them the whole earth was populated. After the flood, Noah lived for three hundred and fifty years.

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Ebilam telele zolɛ hɔle Moli oku baka ne abo wɔ Hyikɛm. Mekɛ ne anu ɛnee Keenama wɔ azɛlɛ ne azo.
translated: The children always play under the big tree near the river. I saw them yesterday afternoon at the same place.

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Mɔɔ Ebilam dwule Yigyibiti la, Yigyibitima nwunle kɛ raalɛ ne anwo yɛ fɛ kpalɛ.
translated: When Ebilam went to Egypt, the Egyptians welcomed him and gave him a place to stay.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Lɔɔto noko mɔɔ nee Ebilam hɔle la, ɔdaye ɔnyianle mboane, mbɔnkye, nlankɛ, yɛɛ ndanlɛ azua.
translated: The food that Ebilam likes includes plantain, cassava, cocoyam, yam, and different kinds of vegetables.

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: Azɛlɛ tɛtɛtɛlɛlɛ a la yɛ nyunlu lɔ la. Te ɛ nwo. Saa ɛkɔ bɛne zo a, mebahɔ fema zo, yɛɛ saa ɛkɔ fema zo noko a, mebahɔ bɛne zo.”
translated: The matter is not as difficult as you think. It is not so. If it were like that, I would have gone there, but if it were like this, I would have come here.

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Lɔɔto dole ɔ nye nleanle azɛlɛ ne na ɔnwunle kɛ Dwɔdan Bɔnza ne anu, toonwo mɔɔ kɔdwu Zowaa la lɛ nzule dɔɔnwo kɛ Ɛdɛnkɛma tola ne anu nee Yigyibiti azɛlɛ zo la.
translated: The car is parked in front of the house and the owner is inside, but later in the evening he will go to the river to meet his friend and they will talk about the Yigyibiti festival.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Sodɔmma le amumuyɛ; bɛyɛle ɛtane mgbole bɛtiale Ɛdɛnkɛma. Ebilam tu ahɔ Hibulɔn
translated: Sodom was destroyed; its people were punished because of their wickedness. Lot fled to Hebron.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Dwazo na kpɔsa kɔ azɛlɛ ne ati nee ɔ bo, kpɔsa nwu azɛlɛ ne tɛtɛlɛ nee ye tendenle, ɔluakɛ mebava meamaa wɔ.”
translated: If you plant a seed and water it at the right time, the seed will grow and become strong, and you will benefit from its fruits.

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Yemɔti Ebilam dudule ye ndanlɛ sua ne na ɔhɔdɛnlanle Mameli oku mbaka ne mɔ mɔɔ wɔ Hibulɔn la anwo. Na ɛkɛ ne, ɔzile azukponle ɔmanle Ɛdɛnkɛma.
translated: We remember Ebilam for his wisdom and strength, for Mameli’s death brought sorrow to the people of Hibulon. Yet, through it, he inspired hope among the people of Edenkema.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: Arelemgbunli nna ɛhye mɔ, Babelonia Belemgbunli Ameleefɛle, Ɛleesaa Belemgbunli Aleyɔko, Yilam Belemgbunli Kɛdɔleewomɛ, yɛɛ Goyem Belemgbunli Taedale,
translated: The Assyrian king is strong, the Babylonian king is wise, the Persian king is brave, the Elamite king is generous, and the Median king is just.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Ye ɛvolɛ bulu nee nna ne anu Belemgbunli Kɛdɔleewomɛ nee arelemgbunli mɔɔ ɔ nee bɛ lɛ konle nu agɔnwolɛyɛlɛ la vale bɛ ɛlɔne hɔhanle Relɛfeeyemma mɔɔ wɔ Ahyetɛlo Kaneeyem, Zuzemma mɔɔ wɔ Ham, yɛɛ Yimemma mɔɔ wɔ Hyeevi Keleateeyem la senle dwɛɛ lile bɛ nwo zo konim.
translated: It was this group that the descendants of Belemgbunli Kɛdɔleewomɛ and Arelemgbunli joined to form the group called Relɛfeeyemma who are at Ahyetɛlo Kaneeyem, the Zuzemma who are at Ham, and the Yimemma who are at Hyeevi Keleateeyem, so that they could all live together as one people.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Yemɔti ɛlɔnema ne sesale Sodɔmma nee Gomɔlama ninyɛne ne kɔsɔɔti nee bɛ aleɛ hɔle.
translated: We remember the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah and what happened to them there.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Eza bɛhyele Lɔɔto, mɔɔ le Ebilam adiema nrenya ne ara nrenya ne, mɔɔ ɛnee de Sodɔm la, na bɛvale ye nee ɔ nwo ninyɛne kɔsɔɔti bɛhɔle.
translated: When Lot left, his wife looked back behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Akee ɔvale ninyɛne ne amuala, ye busuanli Lɔɔto nee ɔ nwo ninyɛne, mraalɛ ne mɔ nee menli mɔɔ bɛhyele bɛ nuokɛ la amuala ɔziale ɔrale. Mɛlekezɛdɛke Yila Ebilam
translated: If someone does not respect his or her parents, the ancestors will not bless him or her, and whatever he or she does will not prosper. This is what Yila Ebilam said.

ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Na Ebilam dele kɛ Ɛdɛnkɛma ɛlɛtendɛ ahile ye bieko kɛ, “Nrenya ɛhye ɛnrɛli wɔ agya. Ɛdawɔ ɛ kunlu ralɛ nrenya a bali wɔ agya a.”
translated: And Ebilam said to Edenkema again that, "A child does not surpass an elder. It is not good for a child to be greater than an elder."

ref: Abram believed the Lord, so the Lord was pleased with him and accepted him. The Lord Makes Another Promise to Abram
text: Ebilam liele Ɛdɛnkɛma lile, na ɛhye manle ɔzɔle Ɛdɛnkɛma anye na ɔliele ye ɔdole nu.
translated: The moon shines at night, and its light helps people see at night when there is no other light.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Saa nloma mɔɔ di nane funli la ba nwolɛ a, ɛnee yevoa bɛ.
translated: If you see that kind of bird walking on the ground, it means rain will fall.

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Ebilam buale ye kɛ, “Ayi, wɔ sonvolɛ raalɛ ne wɔ ɛ sa nu, debie biala mɔɔ fɛta la fa yɛ ye.” Yemɔti Sɛleeyae hilele Heega abodelɛ, na ɔnriandile.
translated: When Ebilam heard this, he said, “No, if you do not return soon, something bad might happen to you.” Seleeyae trusted Heega completely, and he did not hesitate.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Ɛdɛnkɛma soanvolɛ hɔdole ye wɔ ɛsɛlɛ ne azo nzubula bie mɔɔ wɔ Hyulu adenle zo la anwo,
translated: The importance of unity is that it helps people to achieve their goals and live peacefully in the community.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: na ɔbizale ye kɛ, “Sɛleeyae sonvolɛ raalɛ Heega, nienwu a ɛvi wɔra ɛke a, yɛɛ nienwu ɛleka a ɛlɛkɔ a?” Ɔbuale ye kɛ, “Melɛnriandi meavi me menle raalɛ Sɛleeyae anwo.”
translated: And he asked, “Who then is this Sɛleeyae son of Heega, that I should go to him, or what should I do for him?” He replied, “I do not know him and I have never seen this Sɛleeyae before.”

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Ɔbayɛ nrenya mɔɔ ye subane bayɛ kɛ asoaso mɔɔ bɛtɛtetele ye la. Ɔbahyi awie biala, yɛɛ awie biala bahyi ye, na ɔ nee ɔ nwo amra biala ɛnrɛdɛnla.”
translated: Women do not easily forget evil deeds and they remember them for a long time. Every day ends, every day begins again, but she does not forget any wrong done to her.

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Na Heega hanle kɛ, “Asoo amgba menwu Nyamenle anyunlu na metɛte aze?” Na ɔvɛlɛle Ɛdɛnkɛma mɔɔ nee ye dendɛle la, “Nyamenle mɔɔ nwu me la.”
translated: And Heega said, “If I die, will I not see God’s face?” And the angel answered him, “You will not die before you see God.”

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Me nee wɔ bayɛ ngyekyeleɛ, yɛɛ mebamaa ɛ bo zo amra azo dɔɔnwo kpalɛ.”
translated: My mother was very strict, she would beat me if I did something wrong.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava azɛlɛ ne mɔɔ ɛlɛkpɔsakpɔsa zolɛ la meamaa wɔ nee ɛ bo zo amra. Bɛbava Keena azɛlɛ ne kɔsɔɔti bɛayɛ bɛ ɛdeɛ, na mebayɛ bɛ Nyamenle.”
translated: And I will come tomorrow morning to the place where I told you to wait for me. If Keena comes tomorrow and asks for you, tell her, "I have gone to Nyamenle."

translated: The chief and the elders went to the forest to perform the rites.

ref: Shechem was the most respected person in his family, and he was so in love with Dinah that he hurried off to get everything done.
text: Na Hyikɛm angyinla nwolɛ fee, ɔboalekɛ ɔ nye die Gyekɔbo ara raalɛ ne anwo. Hyikɛm le sonla mɔɔ bɛdi ye eni kpalɛ wɔ bɛ abusua ne anu a.
translated: When Hyikɛm was young, he used to play with his friend Gyekɔbo every morning. Hyikɛm was always happy to spend time with his family and friends.

ref: We have to do only one thing before they will agree to stay here and become one nation with us. Our men will have to be circumcised just like theirs.
text: Mɔɔ menli ɛhye mɔ kpondɛ na bɛadɛnla yɛ avinli bɛ nee yɛ ayɛ ko la a le kɛ, yɛ mrenya ne mɔ, ko biala kɛyɛ ye mrenyanu nzonlɛ, kɛmɔ bɛyɛ bɛ ɛdeɛ ne la.
translated: If we want to achieve success in life, we must work hard, be patient, and always strive to improve ourselves, because that is the only way to reach our goals.

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Ye kenle nsa ne azo mɔɔ ɛnee bɛ nganlɛ ne mɔ ɛlɛfofo bɛ la, Gyekɔbo amra ne mɔ anu nwiɔ, Semɛyɔn nee Livae, Daena amediema mrenya ne mɔ, vale bɛ dadeɛ na bɛhɔle bɛ nwo zo wɔ suakpole ne azo arɛlevilɛ nu, na bɛhunle mrenya mɔɔ wɔ sua ne azo la amuala.
translated: They carried gifts and went there, and they all bowed down before him, Joseph’s brothers and his mother, Simeon and Levi, Dinah the only daughter, and they gave him gold and silver and new clothes and gifts in abundance, and they all rejoiced together with him.

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Ɛkɛ ne a Dɛbola, mɔɔ nea Relɛbɛka la wule a. Bɛziele ye wɔ oku baka bie abo wɔ Bɛtɛle nyiakɛ, na bɛvɛlɛle ɛkɛ ne “Ɛzunlɛ oku Baka.”
translated: And it was after Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died. They buried her under the oak below Bethel, and they named it “Oak of Weeping.”

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Yemɔti Relahyɛle wule na bɛziele ye wɔ Yifelate adevinli ne. Ɛkɛ ne a ɛnɛ bɛfɛlɛ ye Bɛtelɛhɛm la.
translated: We are going to register because we belong to the family of David. That is why we are going to Bethlehem.

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Yisɔ gyale Keena mraalɛ. Bɛmɛ a le Hɛtenli Yilɔn ara Eda nee Enaa ara Ɔhɔlebama. Enaa ɔ ze a le Haevaenli Zebɛyɔn,
translated: Keena is a good woman. Her children are Hetenli Yilon, Eda, Enaa, and Oholebama. Enaa's child is Haevaenli Zebeyon.

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lotan abo zo amra a le Hɔlae nee Himan mbusua ne mɔ anu amra.
translated: Lotan killed an animal and gave it to the Hɔlae and Himan families to share and eat.

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: Hɔlae mbusua ekpunli ekpunli mɔɔ wɔ Yidɔm azɛlɛ zo la mgbanyinli a le Lotan, Hyobale, Zebɛyɔn, Enaa, Daehyɔn, Yizɛ, yɛɛ Daehyan. Yidɔm Arelemgbunli (
translated: The chiefs of the clans of the sons of Esau were: the sons of Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These are the chiefs of Edom.

ref: Joseph said, “Let me tell you about my dream.
text: Ɔhanle ɔhilele bɛ kɛ, “Bɛdie ɛlalɛ bie mɔɔ meha la:
translated: The chief said, “Listen carefully to what I am about to say:

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Kenle ko Dwosefi amediema ne mɔ vale bɛ ze mboane ne mɔ hɔle adidi wɔ Hyikɛm.
translated: When it is Friday evening, people gather and eat food at Hyikɛm.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Gyekɔbo zele Dwosefi kɛ, “Ɛ mediema ne mɔ ɛva mboane ne mɔ ɛhɔ adidi wɔ Hyikɛm. Bɛla maa menzoa wɔ bɛ nwo lɔ.” Dwosefi buale kɛ, “Egya, mewɔ ɛke, soa me.”
translated: "Gyekɔbo said to Dwosefi, 'I am hungry and I want to eat some food at Hyikɛm. Please let me go with you.' Dwosefi replied, 'Father, I have some, carry me.'"

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Bɛhanle bɛhilele bɛ nwo ngoko kɛ, “Ahenle ne mɔɔ ka ɛlalɛ ne la a ɛlɛba la.
translated: "The elders have said that, 'The words of the elders are like lamps that light the way.'"

ref: As Joseph's brothers sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were loaded with all kinds of spices that they were taking to Egypt.
text: Akee bɛdɛnlanle aze kɛ bɛdi debie; na bɛmanle bɛ nye zo la, bɛnwunle kɛ Yehyemeeyɛle adendulɛma ekpunli bie ɛlɛba. Bɛvi Geleade, bɛlɛ bɛ afunlumu mɔɔ zozo ɛyɛlɛ, alufuanlɛ, yɛɛ ayile ndɔkɛ bɛlɛkɔ Yigyibiti.
translated: Some will go to the left and others to the right; and those who remain will stay in the town, mourning and remembering the deeds of their ancestors. In Gilead, they will cross the river in fear, trembling, and will finally arrive at Egypt.

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Na Gyekɔbo suosuanle ye tɛladeɛ ne mɔɔ ɔwula la anu, ɔvale ɛrele ɔpɛle ɔ bo, na ɔzunle ɔ ra ne mekɛ dɔɔnwo.
translated: And Gyekɔbo’s wisdom was greater than that of all the elders in the town, he judged cases well, and everyone came to him for advice.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: Eza ɔwole nrenya bieko, na ɔdonle ye Hyila. Dwuda wɔ Akezebe la yɛɛ ɔwole ralɛ nrenya ɛhye a.
translated: If someone does a good deed, he will be blessed. On Monday, at Akezebe, the person will receive a great reward.

ref: Later, Judah chose Tamar as a wife for Er, his oldest son.
text: Na Dwuda gyale raalɛ maanle ɔ ra kpanyinli Ɛɛ. Bɛfɛlɛ raalɛ ne Teemaa.
translated: On Sunday morning, he went to visit his grandfather. The visit was in Tema.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Na Dwuda ara nrenya kpanyinli Ɛɛ le amumuyɛ, yemɔti Ɛdɛnkɛma hunle ye.
translated: And only on Monday will the elders gather for the ceremony, to discuss important matters.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
text: Mekɛ guale nu la Hyua ara raalɛ ne mɔɔ Dwuda gya ye la wule. Ɔwiele ye kunlane ne kyi la, ɔhɔle ɛleka mɔɔ bɛpepɛ ye mboane nwo enrinli la wɔ Temenasuazo. Ɔkɔ la ɔ nee ɔ gɔnwo Haela mɔɔ vi Adɔlam la a hɔle a.
translated: When David heard that Saul and his men were searching for him, he went down to the stronghold. Saul was searching for him every day, but God did not deliver David into his hands. David stayed in the wilderness strongholds and in the hills of the Desert of Ziph. Day after day Saul searched for him, but God did not give David into his hands.

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Mɔɔ bɛtwili ye bɛahɔyela ye la, ɔvale nrɛlaleɛ ɔhɔmanle ɔ hu ɔ ze Dwuda kɛ, “Ahenle mɔɔ lɛ ninyɛne ɛhye mɔ la a nee me ɛlɛnrenzɛ a. Yemɔti mebodo wɔ, nea bɛ kpalɛ na ɛbanwu ahenle mɔɔ kpɔdelera ne, nyɛma ne, yɛɛ kpɔma ne le ye ɛdeɛ la.”
translated: When the elders had gathered and were discussing, a wise man among them stood up and said, "The reason why we are suffering like this is because we have neglected our traditions. If we return to them, respect them, and honor our elders, things will get better for us."

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Yehyemeeyɛlema dɔle Dwosefi la, bɛ nee ye hɔle Yigyibiti, na Yigyibitinli bie mɔɔ bɛfɛlɛ ye Pɔtefaa, mɔɔ le Yigyibiti belemgbunli mgbanyima ne mɔ ko, na eza ɔle suakunlu ɛkɛ ne sinzavolɛma safohyenle la, dɔle Dwosefi vile bɛ ɛkɛ.
translated: We meet every Friday, and we go to the church, and after church we all go to the market, and the church leader gives us advice on how to live well, so every Friday is like that.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: na ɔ menle ne nwunle kɛ Ɛdɛnkɛma li Dwosefi anzi, na ɔmanle debie biala mɔɔ ɔvale ɔ sa ɔhanle ye la yɛle kpalɛ.
translated: And he/she did not eat or drink until the festival of Dwosefi was over, and everyone in the town wondered how he/she was able to endure such hunger.

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: Melɛ tumi wɔ awuke ye anu kɛ ɔdaye ala la, yɛɛ ɔtɛtuale me kɛ memmafa me sa meka debie biala kɛ ɛdawɔ ala, ɔboalekɛ ɔ ye a le wɔ. Duzu ati yɛɛ akee menwɔyɛ amumuyɛ kpole ɛhye, na menwɔyɛ ɛtane menwɔtia Nyamenle ɛ?”
translated: I have the power to do what I want on the Sabbath and any other day, but I discipline myself and do not allow myself to say anything or do anything, unless it is necessary. Sometimes I wonder whether I am doing the right thing or not, and whether I am pleasing or displeasing God.

ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: Yeanvane nrenya ne mɔɔ nea pilizama anwo zo la anwo kɛ ɔkɛnlea debie mɔɔ bɛvale bɛwulale Dwosefi asa anu la, ɔboalekɛ ɛnee Ɛdɛnkɛma nee ye a lua a, yɛɛ debie biala mɔɔ ɔyɛle la, Ɛdɛnkɛma manle ɔyɛle kpalɛ.
translated: We should remember that what happened in the past and what is happening now will help us to prepare for the future, especially on Fridays when we gather, so that everyone will know what to do, and the future will be better for everyone.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Ɛnɛ anwo zo kenle nsa, belemgbunli ne bamaa bɛaraye wɔ wɔ efiade ɛke na bɛava wɔ gyima ne bɛawula ɛ sa nu; ɛbava belemgbunli ne kɔpo ne wɔawula ɔ sa nu, na wɔazu ye nza kɛ dɛba ne ala la.
translated: Today, if you do not wash your hands, diseases and dirt will enter your body through your mouth; diseases and germs will get into your body, and you may fall sick or even die.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Na saa ɔyɛ boɛ ɔmaa wɔ a, anrɛɛ kakye me na yɛ boɛ ɛhye maa me. Mebodo wɔ, bɔ me duma kile belemgbunli ne maa ɔye me ɔvi efiade ɛke.
translated: And when he was angry, he did not shout at me but his anger burned within him. I stayed, doing my work quietly and waited for him to calm down before I approached him.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Ɔluakɛ nɔhalɛ nu, bɛwuale me a bɛ nee me vi Hibulu azɛlɛ zo rale a, yɛɛ ɛke noko, metɛyɛle ɛhwee mɔɔ ɔwɔ kɛ bɛto me efiade a.”
translated: At the time of trouble, help me so that my child Hibulu will not suffer, and if possible, I will not do anything bad to anyone who helps me.

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: Mɔɔ gyi zolɛ kpalɛ anwuma la, aleɛ ngakyile dɔɔnwo mɔɔ bɛdo ye fonlomo nu la wɔ nu mɔɔ mefa meahɔmaa belemgbunli ne a, na ɛnee nloma ɛlɛsuso ye me ti zo ali.”
translated: When I woke up early in the morning, I saw a beautiful bird sitting on my window, and its feathers were shining brightly in the sunlight.

ref: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
text: Ɛvolɛ nwiɔ anzi, Yigyibiti belemgbunli ne hanle ɛlalɛ kɛ ɛnee ɔgyi Azule Naele ne anloa.
translated: When the sun rises, the Egyptians gather at the riverbank and worship the god Azule Naele and the ancestors.

ref: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
text: Ɔnwunle kɛ nlankɛ mgbole, dɔlera nsuu vi azule ne anu ɛvinde na bɛlɛdidi ɛhonle ne mɔɔ wɔ ɛkɛ ne la anu.
translated: The moon and stars shine, giving their light at night so that people can see and move about in the darkness.

ref: The next morning the king was upset. So he called in his magicians and wise men and told them what he had dreamed. None of them could tell him what the dreams meant.
text: Yemɔti aleɛ hyenle la ɛnee ye adwenle ɛzɛkye. Na ɔzoanle ɔmanle bɛhɔvɛlɛle ngonyiama mɔɔ wɔ Yigyibiti la amuala nee maanle ne anu nrɛlɛbɛvolɛma ne amuala na ɔhanle ye ɛlalɛ ne ɔhilele bɛ. Noko bɛ nuhua ko bɔbɔ angola ɛlalɛ ne abo kile kile belemgbunli ne.
translated: We must always remember our history and our thoughts. And the unity of the people is important so that we can build our nation and protect our culture and language for future generations. Everyone should work together to ensure that our language and traditions do not disappear.

ref: A young Hebrew, who was a servant of the captain of the guard, was there with us at the time. When we told him our dreams, he explained what each of them meant,
text: Mekɛ ne anu ɛnee yɛ nee Hibulunli kpavolɛ bie mɔɔ le sinzavolɛma safohyenle ne sonvolɛ la a gua ɛkɛ a. Yɛhanle yɛ ɛlalɛ ne mɔ yɛhilele ye, na ɔhilele ɛlalɛ ne mɔ abo ɔhilele yɛ, kɛmɔ sonla ko biala ye ɛlalɛ ne de la.
translated: If I see his face then I will know that he is the person who helped me at the market yesterday. Sometimes it is difficult to remember people, but when you see their face you can remember them, especially if you have met them before.

ref: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
text: Dwosefi buale kɛ, “Nana, medame me ngome mengola. Nyamenle yɛɛ bahile ɔ bo ahile Nana a.”
translated: "Dwosefi said, 'Nana, I have finished my food and I am satisfied. God has truly blessed you, Nana.'"

ref: The skinny cows ate the fat ones.
text: Na nlankɛ nwanlera, kengamgbaanwo nsuu ne hyele alumua nlankɛ mgbole dɔlera nsuu ne lile.
translated: In the dry season, the scarcity of water makes it difficult for animals to find enough water to drink.

ref: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
text: Noko bɛlile bɛ bɛwiele bɔbɔ la, ɛnee ɛnwu nlankɛ ne mɔ a ɛkɛze kɛ bɛtɛdile ɛhwee, ɔluakɛ ɛnee kɛ bɛde ala la yɛɛ bɛde a. Na mendunwuele.
translated: Some people came and took the things, but there was nothing left for us, so we had to go back without anything. I was not happy.

ref: Joseph replied: Your Majesty, both of your dreams mean the same thing, and in them God has shown what he is going to do.
text: Na Dwosefi buale kɛ, “Nana, ɛlalɛ ne le kokye. Nyamenle ɛye mɔɔ ɔbayɛ la ɛhile Nana.
translated: And Dwosefi said, "Chief, this is the truth. God is the one who has made this possible for you, Chief."

ref: For seven years Egypt will have more than enough grain,
text: Ɛvolɛ nsuu bara mɔɔ aleɛ babu kpole wɔ Yigyibiti azɛlɛ ye azo a.
translated: Heavy rains often cause floods in some areas of the Western Region.

ref: God has given you two dreams to let you know that he has definitely decided to do this and that he will do it soon.
text: Ɛlalɛ fane nwiɔ ne mɔɔ Nana hanle la kile kɛ Nyamenle ɛbɔ ye kpɔkɛ ɛwie, na ɔnrɛhyɛ biala na yeazi.
translated: That is why we should fear and respect the elders, because God created them first, and we should not insult or despise them.

ref: Then appoint some other officials to collect one fifth of every crop harvested in Egypt during the seven years when there is plenty.
text: Nana ɛbɔ ɔ bo ɛyeye menli mɔɔ banlea azɛlɛ ne mɔ azo la, na ɔlie aleɛ biala mɔɔ kɛvinde Yigyibiti azɛlɛ ye azo la anu ngyɛnu nnu ɛkpɔ ko wɔ ɛvolɛ nsuu ne mɔɔ aleɛ babu la anu.
translated: Nana says it is good for people to keep their surroundings clean, and he also advises everyone to always wash their hands with soap and clean water before eating food.

ref: So the king said to them, “Who could possibly handle this better than Joseph? After all, the Spirit of God is with him.”
text: Na ɔzele ye mgbanyima ne mɔ kɛ, “Bɛnea a awie bie wɔ ɛkɛ tɛla ɛhye mɔɔ Nyamenle Sunsum ne wɔ ye nu la ɔ?”
translated: And the elder asked him, “How does the end differ when it is under the influence of the Holy Spirit and when it is not?”
